post.description: This endpoint is used to create activities in a specified portfolio. Each transaction from a broker should be translated to one activity (e.g. Buy, Sell, TransferIn, TransferOut).

get.description: This endpoint is used to fetch activities from a specified portfolio.

parameters.name: activityType
parameters.description: If provided, only activities of the specified type(s) will be returned. If not provided, all activity types will be returned.

parameters.name: assetType
parameters.description: If provided, only activities for holdings of the specified asset type(s) will be returned. If not provided, all activities are returned

parameters.name: holdingId
parameters.description: If provided, only activities for holdings of the specified id(s) will be returned. If not provided, all activities are returned

get.description: Retrieve a list of Portfolios that the user has granted read access to

post.description:
Create a new Portfolio for the user. 

Once you have created a Portfolio, you can perform read and write operations on it using the Portfolio Id returned in the response.

Create a new custom holding for the user. 

Once you have created a custom holding, you can create activities by using the create activities endpoint.
Import quotes for Custom Asset holdings

Create a new real estate holding for the user. 

Once you have created a real estate holding, you can create activities by using the create activities endpoint.

Create a new cash holding for the user. 

Once you have created a cash holding, you can create activities by using the create activities endpoint.

Create a new commodity holding for the user. 

Once you have created a commodity holding, you can create activities by using the create activities endpoint.
This endpoint is used to fetch performance data from the specified portfolios.

info.title: Parqet Connect API
info.description:
The **Parqet Connect API** empowers developers to seamlessly integrate external financial data and tools with the Parqet portfolio tracking ecosystem.

### Key Capabilities

* **User Integration**: Retrieve connection status and permissions for the authenticated user.
* **Portfolio Management**: Programmatically create new portfolios and retrieve metadata for existing ones.
* **Activity Syncing**: Push and pull investment activities. The API supports a wide range of transaction types (Buy, Sell, Dividends, Fees, Transfers) and automatically handles:
  * **Securities**: Identified via ISIN (Stocks, ETFs).
  * **Cryptocurrencies**: Identified via Ticker Symbols.

### Authentication

This API uses OAuth2. Access is granularly controlled via scopes:

* `portfolio:read`: Read access to portfolio data.
* `portfolio:write`: Write access to portfolio data (creating portfolios and activities).

anyOf.description: The User is active and all connect api calls are expected to suceeed
The User has deleted their connect account. All other connect api calls will fail with a 410 Gone error. After 30 days the user data is permanently deleted.

imageData.description: Base64 encoded PNG image data (256x256, max 512KiB)

referenceAccountFor.description:
Defines the asset classes for which this cash holding acts as a reference account.

For activities in the selected classes, Parqet creates automatic counter-bookings:
buy activities are debited from this account, while sell activities and dividends are credited.

If no value is provided, the cash holding is independent and not used as a reference account.

anyOf.description: The true time weighted rate of return.

fees.description: Sum of all fees that were paid during the interval.

taxes.description: Sum of all taxes that were paid during the interval.

gainGross.description:
Describes the difference between gross invested capital and asset value during the interval in `currency`.
Purchases and sales have an effect on invested capital and total asset value, so they also
affect the unrealized gains.
Careful: The value of the asset at the start of the interval is treated as invested capital - only
asset gains _during_ the interval are regarded.

gainNet.description:
Describes the difference between net invested capital and asset value during the interval in `currency`.
Only purchase costs are considered, taxes paid on sales are ignored.
Careful: The value of the asset at the start of the interval is treated as invested capital - only
asset gains _during_ the interval are regarded.

returnGross.description:
Describes the difference between gross invested capital and asset value during the interval in percent.
Purchases and sales have an effect on invested capital and total asset value, so they also
affect the unrealized gains.
Careful: The value of the asset at the start of the interval is treated as invested capital - only
asset gains _during_ the interval are regarded.

returnNet.description:
Describes the difference between net invested capital and asset value during the interval in percent.
Purchases and sales have an effect on invested capital and total asset value, so they also
affect the unrealized gains.
Careful: The value of the asset at the start of the interval is treated as invested capital - only
asset gains _during_ the interval are regarded.

unrealizedGains.description: Describes the gains due to quote-price changes.

gainGross.description: Sum of all gains that were realized during the interval. Taxes and fees excluded.

gainNet.description: Sum of all gains that were realized during the interval. Taxes and fees included.

returnGross.description:
Gains realized during the interval in relation to the capital that was originally
invested into the shares that were sold.
Expressed in percent.
Taxes and fees excluded. For reference accounts, the invested capital is net.

returnNet.description:
Gains realized during the interval in relation to the capital that was originally
invested into the shares that were sold.
Expressed in percent.
Taxes and fees included.

gainGross.description: Sum of all dividends received during the interval. Taxes and fees excluded.

gainNet.description: Sum of all dividends received during the interval. Taxes and fees included.

taxes.description: Sum of all taxes paid on dividends that were received during the interval.

fees.description: Sum of all fees paid on dividends that were received during the interval.

anyOf.description: The true time weighted rate of return.

fees.description: Sum of all fees that were paid during the interval.

taxes.description: Sum of all taxes that were paid during the interval.

gainGross.description: Sum of all gains that were realized during the interval. Taxes and fees excluded.

gainNet.description: Sum of all gains that were realized during the interval. Taxes and fees included.

returnGross.description:

returnNet.description:
Gains realized during the interval in relation to the capital that was originally
invested into the shares that were sold.
Expressed in percent.
Taxes and fees included.

gainGross.description: Sum of all dividends received during the interval. Taxes and fees excluded.

gainNet.description: Sum of all dividends received during the interval. Taxes and fees included.

taxes.description: Sum of all taxes paid on dividends that were received during the interval.

fees.description: Sum of all fees paid on dividends that were received during the interval.

shares.description: Total amount of shares that the current holding encompasses

purchasePrice.description: The price of a single share at the time of purchase.

purchaseValue.description: The total amount that was used to purchased the amount of shares for the current holding.

currentPrice.description: The price of a single share right now.

currentValue.description: The total amount that the shares are worth right now.

isSold.description: Is true when all shares of the holding are sold.

net.description:
Net total value of the portfolio, i.e. sum of positive and negative holdings. 
E.g. if you have 10k in stock and 4k in debt on your cash account, the net total is 6k.

base.description:
Absolute value of either the positive or negative holdings, whichever is larger.
E.g. if you have 10k in stocks and 4k in debt on your cash account, the base is 10k.
If you have 5k in stocks and 20k in debt, the base is 20k.

anyOf.description: If mark is "bod", the values are taken at the begin of day of the `date` timestamp.
If mark is "eod", the values are taken at the end of day of the `date` timestamp.
If mark is "most_recent", the values are the most recent values on the day of the `date` timestamp. Only present when `date` is today. Not present when dealing with intraday charts.
If mark is "exact_date", the `date` timestamp can be taken literally. The values are taken at the exact millisecond. Only present for intraday charts.

history.description: Total market value of the portfolio at this point in time.

capitalHistory.description: Total capital contributed by the user up to this point (net invested amount).

perfHistory.description:
Total performance in percent for the selected interval. 
Includes unrealized P/L, realized gains, and dividends.

perfHistoryUnrealized.description:
Unrealized performance in percent for the selected interval.
Excludes realized gains and dividends.

ttwror.description: True Time-Weighted Rate of Return (TTWROR), cumulative in percent. Designed to minimize distortion from deposits and withdrawals.

drawdown.description: Drawdown in percent from the previous peak value. 0 means at peak, negative values mean below peak.
